perturbation.original_text: THIS AGREEMENT made this 5th day of December, 2007, between R. C. Boyd Enterprises, LLC, a Texas limited liability company, whose principal place of business is located at 2003 Navasota Cove, Westlake, Texas, referred to in this Agreement as "Company" or "Boyd", and Cano Petroleum, Inc., a Delaware corporation qualified to transact business in Texas, whose principal place of business is located at 801 Cherry Street, Suite 3200, Fort Worth, Texas 76102, referred to in this agreement as "Sponsor" or "Cano".
perturbation.changed_text: THIS AGREEMENT made this 5th day of December, 2007, between R. C. Boyd Enterprises, LLC, a Texas limited liability company, whose principal place of business is located at 2003 Navasota Cove, Westlake, Texas, referred to in this Agreement as "Company" or "Boyd", and Cano Petroleum, Inc., a Delaware corporation qualified to transact business in Texas, whose principal place of business is located at 801 Cherry Street, Suite 3200, Fort Worth, Texas 76102, referred to in this agreement as "Advertiser" or "Cano".
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Sponsor' has been replaced with 'Advertiser'. This creates potential ambiguity later in the document where both terms might be used. It's unclear if 'Sponsor' and 'Advertiser' refer to the same entity, which is Cano. If not, it introduces confusion about which party is bound by obligations assigned to either term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Lead Sponsorship. The Company agrees that Cano shall be identified as the lead sponsor, by having a thirty second lead-in promotion at the beginning of each episode; a thirty second trailer promotion at the end of each episode; and two thirty second commercials during each episode.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Sponsor' to 'Advertiser' creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Cano' as the 'lead sponsor' and assigns it specific responsibilities related to sponsorship. By changing the term used to describe Cano to 'Advertiser' in the initial definition, it introduces ambiguity as to whether the obligations assigned to 'Sponsor' still apply to Cano. The contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably ('Sponsor' and 'Advertiser') without clearly defining if they refer to the same entity, which aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. This ambiguity creates a direct conflict and makes the contract internally inconsistent because it's unclear if Cano has the sponsorship obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year, commencing January 1, 2008 and ending on December 31, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) calendar year, commencing January 1, 2008 and expiring December 31, 2009.
perturbation.explanation: The term end date has been modified from December 31, 2008, to December 31, 2009. This alteration creates an in-text contradiction with the original duration. Now the contract states both a one-year term and a term expiring two years later, leading to uncertainty regarding the actual length of the agreement and the obligations of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Renewal. The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) calendar year, commencing January 1, 2008 and expiring December 31, 2009.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the end date of the agreement from December 31, 2008 to December 31, 2009. The contradicted text states 'Term and Renewal. The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) calendar year, commencing January 1, 2008 and expiring December 31, 2009.' This section already contains the term expiring on December 31, 2009, and therefore the changed text does not contradict it. The explanation states the term length and end date do not match, but in this case, the contradictory term is already written in the 'Contradicted Text', so the change does not create a new contradiction in the agreement, it merely makes the two similar, which is not a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $150,000.00 (One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $37,500.00 (Thirty Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $160,000.00 (One Hundred Sixty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $40,000.00 (Forty Thousand Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008.
perturbation.explanation: The compensation amount has been changed from $150,000 to $160,000. The payments were $37,500.00 and now is $40,000.00. This change directly contradicts the initially stated compensation and installment values, leading to financial disagreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment of Money. In consideration of the services to be rendered under this Agreement as set forth above, the Company shall be entitled to compensation in the amount of $160,000.00 (One Hundred Sixty Thousand Dollars), to be paid in equal installments of $40,000.00 (Forty Thousand Dollars) on January 1, 2008, April 1, 2008, July 1, 2008 and October 1, 2008. If Cano fails to make any payment when required by this Agreement, the Company shall have the option of canceling this Agreement following the passage of ten (10) days after having given Cano written notice of its default. While the Company shall have no further obligation to Cano following cancellation of the Agreement, Cano shall remain liable for all unpaid installments for the then current term of the Agreement, which installments shall be immediately due and payable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the compensation amount from $150,000 to $160,000, with corresponding adjustments to installment payments. The contradicted text also reflects the $160,000 compensation and $40,000 installment amounts. Therefore, the changed text does not contradict the contradicted text. Instead, it aligns with it, likely representing a correction or update applied consistently across the document.
